Class AbstractGLFWWindowSurface

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.graphics.impl.surface.AbstractGLFWWindowSurface
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable, RenderingSurface, WindowSurface, NonThrowingAutoCloseable
Direct Known Subclasses:
GL_GLFWWindowSurface
public abstract class AbstractGLFWWindowSurface extends Object implements WindowSurface
A GLFW window rendering surface, providing base GLFW functionality, except framebuffer-related methods.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface RenderingSurface

    RenderingSurface.Feature, RenderingSurface.ResizeListener

    Returns the height of this surface, in pixels.

    Returns the name of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.NAMEABLE is supported.

    Returns position of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.

    Returns a vector representing dimensions of this surface, in pixels.

    Returns the width of this surface, in pixels.

    Returns X coordinate of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.

    Returns Y coordinate of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.

    Hides this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.HIDEABLE is supported.

    Returns whether a close was explicitly requested with RenderingSurface.setShouldClose() and clears this flag.

    Checks whether this surface is in fullscreen mode, if RenderingSurface.Feature.FULLSCREENABLE is supported.

    Checks whether this surface is hidden, if RenderingSurface.Feature.HIDEABLE is supported.

    Checks whether this surface is resizable, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported.
    boolean

    Checks whether this surface is translucent (only the rendered content is visible).

    Requests this surface to close; in other words, makes RenderingSurface.shouldClose() return true on the next call.

    Sets the new size for this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported.

      pollEvents in interface RenderingSurface

    • shouldClose

      public boolean shouldClose()
      Description copied from interface: RenderingSurface
      Check if this surface should close and clears this flag.

      A close may be either:

      • explicit: triggered by RenderingSurface.setShouldClose()
      • implicit: triggered externally (e.g. window 'X' button or Alt+F4)
      Use RenderingSurface.isExplicitShouldClose() to distinguish between them.
      Specified by:
      shouldClose in interface RenderingSurface
      Returns:
      whether a close was requested

    • setShouldClose

      public void setShouldClose()
      Description copied from interface: RenderingSurface
      Requests this surface to close; in other words, makes RenderingSurface.shouldClose() return true on the next call.
      Specified by:
      setShouldClose in interface RenderingSurface

    • isExplicitShouldClose

      public boolean isExplicitShouldClose()
      Description copied from interface: RenderingSurface
      Returns whether a close was explicitly requested with RenderingSurface.setShouldClose() and clears this flag.

      Used by the engine for headful surfaces that might be closed implicitly (e.g. with window 'X' button).

      Specified by:
      isExplicitShouldClose in interface RenderingSurface
      Returns:
      whether closing has been explicitly requested
      See Also:
      • CloseRequestedEvent
      • RenderingSurface.shouldClose()

    • setResizable

      public void setResizable(boolean resizable)
      Description copied from interface: RenderingSurface
      Changes the resizable state of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported. Surfaces that don't support this feature usually ignore calls to this method.
      Specified by:
      setResizable in interface RenderingSurface
      Specified by:
      setResizable in interface WindowSurface
      Parameters:
      resizable - whether this surface must be resizable

    • isResizable

      public boolean isResizable()
      Description copied from interface: RenderingSurface
      Checks whether this surface is resizable, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported. Surfaces that don't support this feature must always return false here.
      Specified by:
      isResizable in interface RenderingSurface
      Specified by:
      isResizable in interface WindowSurface
      Returns:
      whether this surface is resizable

    • onResize

      public void onResize(RenderingSurface.ResizeListener callback)
      Description copied from interface: RenderingSurface
      Adds a resize listener to this surface.

      It may be useful to check the resizable state before adding a callback. Surfaces that are never resizable are free to ignore calls to this method.

      Specified by:
      onResize in interface RenderingSurface
      Specified by:
      onResize in interface WindowSurface
      Parameters:
      callback - the listener to call every time this surface gets resized

    • setSize

      public void setSize(int newWidth, int newHeight)
      Description copied from interface: RenderingSurface
      Sets the new size for this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported.
      Specified by:
      setSize in interface RenderingSurface
      Specified by:
      setSize in interface WindowSurface
      Parameters:
      newWidth - the new width
      newHeight - the new height
      See Also:

      Specified by:

      Specified by:
      setFullscreen in interface RenderingSurface
      Specified by:
      setFullscreen in interface WindowSurface
      Parameters:
      fullscreen - whether this surface must be in fullscreen mode

    • isTranslucent

      public boolean isTranslucent()
      Description copied from interface: RenderingSurface
      Checks whether this surface is translucent (only the rendered content is visible). This is, in general, determined at creation time and may not change later.
      Specified by:
      isTranslucent in interface RenderingSurface
      Specified by:
      isTranslucent in interface WindowSurface
      Returns:
      whether this surface is translucent

    • close

      public void close()
      Description copied from interface: RenderingSurface
      This method is not expected to throw any checked exceptions.

      Note: surfaces must not be closed manually; this is handled by the graphics manager. Use RenderingSurface.setShouldClose() instead to signal a close request.

      After calling this method, the surface instance should not be used. Calling this method more than once does not have any effect.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface NonThrowingAutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface RenderingSurface

    • ensureOpen

      protected void ensureOpen()

      protected void ensureOnCorrectThread()
